mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals an uptick in consumer demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining INDPRO are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with rising FEDFUNDS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) signals a labor market shock that raises uncertainty, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in real disposable personal income (GDPC1) signals an uptick in consumer demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with rising GDPC1 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 1-standard-deviation increase in real personal income (RSAFS) signal a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with declining INDPRO and rising RSAFS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and a 1-standard-deviation decline in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signal a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with rising FEDFUNDS and declining CPIAUCSL are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) and a 1-standard-deviation decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal a labor market shock that raises uncertainty, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE and declining PAYEMS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
